mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, which boosts household disposable income and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of industrial production and housing starts captures the balance between manufacturing capacity and construction demand. When INDPRO rises while HOUST declines, it signals a rotation from housing to industrial activity, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO change minus HOUST change) shows no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across three independent market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly rising consumer prices force the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy tightening. This mechanism predicts that when CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.4 percent month-over-month, the resulting rate hike expectations compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of triggered episodes despite rising CPI and FEDFUNDS, and the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52 percent over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth serves as a continuous leading indicator of aggregate corporate earnings. A positive quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 mechanically supports higher profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon without requiring a specific threshold.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between GDPC1 quarterly changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.05 across multiple decades.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained surge in housing starts above 2000 thousand units signals strong consumer confidence and wealth effects. This threshold activation predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as construction spending and related durable goods demand lift corporate revenues.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 65 percent of months where HOUST remains above 2000 thousand units for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between employment growth and consumer credit expansion captures consumption sustainability. When PAYEMS rises while RSAFS falls, it indicates households are deleveraging while earning more, which historically reduces near-term consumer spending shocks and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The cross-sectional ranking of the PAYEMS-RSAFS spread fails to produce a positive Rank IC above 0.02 for 5-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, rising industrial production coincides with a stable unemployment rate, indicating that supply constraints are binding before demand collapses. This regime-specific signal predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as input cost inflation compresses corporate margins.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and flat UNRATE does not produce a statistically significant negative skew in 20-day forward returns during identified late-cycle periods (p > 0.05).
